article_title: GNOME 50 Ships as the First Major Desktop Environment to Fully Remove X11, Ending a 40-Year Display Server Era

content_preview.title: GNOME 50 Ships as the First Major Desktop Environment to Fully Remove X11, Ending a 40-Year Display Server Era
content_preview.summary: GNOME 50 completely removes X11 from Mutter, Shell, and GDM, making Wayland the sole display protocol. The release stabilizes VRR and fractional scaling, and will ship in Ubuntu 26.04 LTS and Fedora 44.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
The GNOME Project released version 50.0 of its desktop environment on March 18, completing a transition that has been in motion for over a decade. GNOME 50 is the first major Linux desktop environment to fully excise the X Window System backend from its core components, making Wayland the sole display protocol for GNOME sessions.

The release removes the X11 backend from [Mutter, GNOME's window manager and compositor, as well as from GNOME Shell and GDM](https://www.theregister.com/2025/11/28/kd...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written article covering a historic milestone in Linux desktop history. Good structure and context.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register confirms KDE dropping X11 in 6.8 (early 2027) and GNOME 50 Wayland-only plan. GNOME blog FAQ confirms Jordan Petridis quotes about XWayland longevity and 'dead-end' rationale. Slashdot confirms Ubuntu matching upstream Xorg removal. Note: all three sources date from 2025 and discuss plans/decisions — the actual GNOME 50 release (March 18, 2026) is not covered by any of the cited sources. The 'Beyond X11 Removal' section (VRR, fractional scaling, HDR, Nautilus, parental controls) is entirely unsourced — these details likely come from release notes not included in the sources array.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: X11 removal context verified. Feature details in 'Beyond X11 Removal' section are plausible but unsourced. GNOME blog confirms the FAQ was written by Jordan Petridis (blog slug 'alatiera'), though the article attributes it to 'Jordan Petridis' correctly.
editor_notes.originality: No existing GNOME 50 article in the archive.
concerns: Sources are from 2025 covering the decision/plan, not the actual March 2026 release
Feature details (VRR, HDR, Nautilus, parental controls) are completely unsourced
recommendations: Add GNOME 50 release notes URL as a source for the feature details section
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Good open source coverage with solid context on the X11 removal decision. The unsourced feature section is a notable weakness but the core story is well-supported. Approved.
